notes: Multi-model benchmark: the same 3 evals were run on both claude-sonnet-4-6 and claude-opus-4-7 (1 run per eval per configuration). Per-model stats live in run_summary_by_model; the top-level run_summary mirrors the latest model (claude-opus-4-7) for compatibility with the single-model aggregate_benchmark.py script.

On claude-sonnet-4-6 the skill produced +13% pass-rate lift, concentrated in eval 2 (baseline 3/5 vs. with-skill 5/5) on multi-target routing and plan-before-apply. On claude-opus-4-7 the baseline reaches all 15 assertions on its own — lift collapses to +0% on this model, suggesting the current eval suite no longer discriminates skill value for Opus-tier baselines.
Self-graded by executor for the Opus 4.7 run — no separate analyzer pass. Sonnet 4.6 runs used the original narrated/simulated grading methodology described in the prior benchmark.md.
